title: Git Mental Model, Setup, Help, and Repository Safety · Spiral Review
summary: Adopt an unfamiliar repository without overwriting identity, hooks, ignored files, or team policy. Learners retrieve rules and procedures before seeing feedback. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for a repository orientation and configuration evidence notebook after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Explain Git history as content-addressed snapshots connected by parent links and named references.
Distinguish working tree, Git directory, common directory, bare repository, linked worktree, and nested repository boundaries.
Inspect system, global, local, worktree, command, and environment configuration with origin and scope evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# git-basics cumulative repository evidence notebook
# Git operations change only disposable simulated repository state.
# Inspect status, diffs, graph, refs, and recovery before every mutation.

steps.title: Retrieve the complete evidence cycle
steps.instruction: Order the Snapshots Graph evidence cycle for this review task before implementing it.
steps.why: Correct order keeps Snapshots Graph assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward.

content.text: Repository /home/learner/lab uses branch main, local author scope, and Git 4.x. Identify working tree, Git directory, config origins, installed help, and recovery reference before adopting it.

content.title: Ordering constraint
content.text: Order work so Snapshots Graph has a declared contract before execution and a boundary or counterexample after it.

hints: Name the Snapshots Graph precondition or invariant that the review case must preserve.

Trace this concrete case before choosing: Repository /home/learner/lab uses branch main, local author scope, and Git 4.x. Identify working tree, Git directory, config origins, installed help, and recovery reference before adopting it.
Use the documented misconception as a counterexample target: Git stores only line-by-line differences between every adjacent version.

options.text: Inspect repository boundary, config origin, current operation, worktree, index, refs, and graph
Preserve uncommitted work and a recovery ref, then state intended tree and topology
Preview and perform the least destructive simulated Git operation
Verify status, staged and range diffs, graph, tests, remote target, and recovery path

stimulus.caption: Three excerpts from a learner evidence packet; only one connects a claim to a reproducible check.

lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Use installed help, version evidence, dry runs, status, and backups before following repository-changing instructions. Artifact under review: a repository orientation and configuration evidence notebook.

options.text: Apply the visible technique immediately, omit assumptions, and treat one successful output as complete proof.

The record says the result looks right but supplies no quantity, trace, condition, or counterexample.

steps.title: Record what required effort
steps.instruction: Write the review evidence note for Help Version Safety: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — Repository /home/learner/lab uses branch main, local author scope, and Git 6.x. Identify working tree, Git directory, config origins, installed help, and recovery reference before adopting it.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Use installed help, version evidence, dry runs, status, and backups before following repository-changing instructions.

checks.description: The evidence cycle runs from problem framing through changed-case validation.
checks.failureMessage: Frame the decision before modeling, and validate after obtaining a result.
